hawkish_phrases: inflation was projected to weaken this year
inflation had been running below the Committee's 2 percent longer-run objective
market-based measures of inflation compensation remained very low
the overall effect of the outbreak on prices was seen as disinflationary
the return of inflation to the Committee's 2 percent longer-run objective would likely be further delayed
dovish_phrases: causing tremendous human and economic hardship across the United States and around the world
sharp declines in economic activity and a surge in job losses
the second quarter would likely see overall economic activity decline at an unprecedented rate
the unemployment rate would soon reach the highest levels of the post–World War II period
expects to maintain this target range until it is confident that the economy has weathered recent events
summary: The Committee unanimously maintained the federal funds rate at 0-0.25% with forward guidance to hold until confident the economy has weathered recent events, amid unprecedented economic contraction and extreme dovish concerns about employment and growth during the COVID-19 crisis.
